name: Jenna Burns
subject: Fani Willis’s crusade to rip my family apart
body:
Dear Patriot,

If the name Fani Willis sounds familiar to you, there's a reason.

She's the radical District Attorney in Georgia who's been trying to put President Trump behind bars.

But President Trump isn't the only person Fani Willis is abusing her office to target.

She's also trying to lock my husband away for the _rest_ _of_ _his_ _life_ for doing his job as a police officer and trying to keep our city safe.

**[Before I ask for your help, let me start at the beginning...](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGa2wB3-Z35g1gdqLVhgKOFnKg7Z-xdRJaVkyeKRY5y6HKsJvDh-9obNGGNOs34HmaSxFBuN5K50v7UoqrP_W7xqOJA76Dnw1dN6NQZTHb6X1f51icxW9KSpzHtZlprHtsomtGFTGQsdEGB3LzjjXpy-)**

My husband, James, served in the Atlanta Police Department. That's me, James, and our son in the photo I sent.
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGYBMJr3RLbEuVeCTJQ5VPSKnP1NZoQgK_LUcI-EqoIKuu3Z5VQca1Mc5u38McN7n0uyIVpnTCtM-2bkISfH9KFSSPX7CIc-eoG2KWgixYiKuxJ8XpIJ_KeuDMajSTo6onKXYRe_a6DshCpA2yOhhMZf)
Being a police officer meant the world to James. He was born and raised in Atlanta, and saw his job as a way to help his neighbors. But if Fani Willis has her way... **_life in prison_** could be his reward.

Our nightmare started more than eight years ago, when an off-duty police officer radioed in about a man breaking into a parked car at an apartment complex.

Then the officer stopped responding. Dispatch issued a distress call, and all available units rushed over.

My husband was closest to the scene, so he arrived first. When he got there, he saw a running car parked facing the wrong way on a one-way street. With his emergency lights on, James got out to investigate.

That's when it happened.

The suspect saw my husband, slammed on the gas, and drove straight at him.

James was certain the man was intentionally trying to run him over.

With only a split second to think, James drew his weapon and fired a single shot at the vehicle bearing down on him. It struck the suspect, who died instantly.

James was shaken by the incident, but thankfully he was unhurt. He went home, certain he had done everything by the book. **So you can imagine our shock when James found out he was being charged with murder!**

James was forced to turn himself in, and spent the night locked up in jail in solitary confinement. They treated him like a common criminal. They even kept him in a straitjacket!

We all thought this was a giant misunderstanding -- just a soft-on-crime District Attorney trying to make a name for themselves by targeting a police officer.

We couldn't have been more wrong.

You see, James is white, and the suspect he shot was black. So the woke, anti-police prosecutors in Atlanta have made it their mission to destroy my husband.

For eight years now, these radical prosecutors -- and the woke mob who helped get them elected -- have dragged my husband's name through the mud.

The District Attorney at the time held press conferences bragging about how tough they were for going after my husband. They even campaigned on putting my James behind bars!

Every time James has to appear at the Fulton County Courthouse for a hearing, angry protesters show up carrying giant signs with James' mugshot on them, with “MURDERER” scribbled across his face in red spray paint.

Now you understand why I'm so upset and why **[I'm turning to you for help.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGaIq9RfqrKAnAZ7lkuLHa7J-BZO8aLH9UzqFxEvrCHhMA7Ekfs7KkpMZxBmQPgEokCUu9mxf1hRLvdVceKO-NMZF-xCYQ6YPyk0vGG7-U4FnHc7Qg4qANSEUoa8hOdMsvzboISs2nz8m6aZItbQ9B2U)**

James is an honest, hardworking, good-hearted man who only wants to help people.

He's the guy everyone calls when a broken faucet floods their kitchen, or when their car breaks down.

When he was younger, he spent years traveling to dangerous Third-World countries to help needy people -- places like South Sudan, Madagascar, Liberia, and Haiti. And it was that same selflessness that drove James to become a police officer and graduate the police academy near the top of his class.

My husband is _exactly_ the kind of man you want keeping your city safe.

But none of that matters to radical prosecutors like Fani Willis. She _knows_ what happened that night. She _knows_ James was in a life-or-death situation. She _knows_ that the charge should have been dropped years ago.

But Fani Willis doesn't care.

She just wants to make an example out of my husband. For years, we've been trapped in limbo.

James' police department just threw us away. There's supposed to be this bond of brotherhood in law enforcement. But his fellow officers were forbidden to talk to him -- and the higher-ups fired him before the investigation was even finished.

We've lost nearly all our friends, and we've even been forced to move because of the terrible lies the prosecutors have been spreading about my husband.

It's been nearly impossible for James to get a job with these charges hanging over him.

By some miracle, we've been able to get by, but every day is difficult. And what's been truly overwhelming is the cost of James' legal defense.

My friend, we aren't wealthy people. James' father had to empty his retirement account. Other relatives have sold family heirlooms. We've put nearly every penny we can scrape together into this case.

And it still hasn't been enough.

I know this is how Fani Willis and the radical prosecutors in her office are trying to punish my husband. They're dragging this out as long as possible to break our family.

Earlier this year, we ran out of money. James was about to turn to a public defender. We had all but given up hope... **And then we got a call from Jason Johnson, the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund, or LELDF.**

If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongfully-accused police officers like James.

LELDF has helped dozens of innocent officers -- and their families – at their lowest moment.

And we _were_ at our lowest moment!

Not only did the LELDF board decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best attorneys so James can finally clear his name.

That's why I'm writing to you today to ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone...

**[Could you send a contribution today to help defend James?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGaMAcQoc-4CU8xwu3QwcUYsX3Q4Rmm4X0Xlq4vzh_1dF4YSQ4WSwxr5tSUJ-geJ6__fSTViUJ83j8dCvgYI7X_jm-17qXUDeLxwYy82pPiR2Zl2x-Bms334G1-L1qGYKsJ4vV9UQ1_uyHrArAafVf7O)**

His legal team needs to be working day and night to get ready to defeat Fani Willis's team and get my husband declared NOT GUILTY once and for all.

I know that times are tough right now. And if we had any other options, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for help from a stranger.

But like I mentioned earlier, we have nowhere else to turn. And I'm so scared for what will happen to my husband.

You know how police officers are treated behind bars. If James is convicted, it's basically a death sentence.

**[So, if you're at all able, would you consider making a tax-deductible donation of $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or $1,000 to LELDF to help wrongfully-accused officers like James?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGb8-aNBPxLZL-OsAZ_-TJHqijOU9zemC_ovry6O9ezDW8hq0SYs85QFGvFU1r9vE6XiSREP-QJKjB9KaigxCRprjPWXG_Wl0fdT5G_cwvdrqNeQ8gV3pcy-RFKNoZoZShcSS3u_GJjnIOc5pBBSlRtV)**

LELDF is a non-profit organization. So your gift is tax-deductible.

And it's your **[$35 gift](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGZBTEk91Nf8Z2Q-1IWv1mJmt5H7oNeXiJ3ZwiKKnojijmfUxq43sz_s20GV7H3iB4b5oR5HaXVGzbXNiY3wLLUjpNZ1l3qmw-J5thvQrpFJC7l0E7evkV7Ss2x_lt-KX5sEI5e78XkkRnxbEZfi94sZ)** that could make the difference between defeating Fani Willis and the radical anti-police prosecutors on her team -- and my husband spending the _rest_ _of_ _his_ _life_ in prison.

I'm sorry this e-mail turned out so long. Thank you for taking the time to read about my husband. And thank you in advance for helping my James.

Sincerely,
Jenna Burns

P.S. My husband did EVERYTHING he needed to survive that night. He doesn't deserve to die in prison. But that's EXACTLY what will happen if radical prosecutor Fani Willis gets her way. Please help James before it's too late! **[Follow this link right now to make your best tax-deductible gift of $35 or more to LELDF.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69822d3762e248768c329012e76b2176&_e=KAMWgh2lMfCyrpUMPw7xMwgqu7deJrnlVfhwTOhUzAhPkSPvbhmG8KwMT2RWpl-fG_vMAyxDDSN2NDYnkA6mC01S_LIdfApqCKgyEpUW4vYoyheIXo6mJQcvtxbjphdO39JxjzCybzGP18JhTV_TySxU3b3WNhbTkZ-fL2UYXt48w9i8nOsxsqzVg9mmV8GDr6iWE9opCyAyes6-Zg3a27sfzU0xCnGwuqn8E_DjWGYgkZW4eANNzLaiXu3feSuotTcy19hNTvfxYEhCQ7VPGP99QEGjMIWVYdU8ZCcLfiiiJlo4lUIPiHuQ92yjqW0Ms-yRxstoSHFSx3FotA94WR2KmhEDxHGu5i7b1vykr_TASxHF155hXAVSBM7HgwxV)** Thank you for helping me save my husband and my family.
--------

_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit**www.PoliceDefense.org**. And remember -Blue Lives Matter!_

clean_body: Dear Patriot, If the name Fani Willis sounds familiar to you, there's a reason. She's the radical District Attorney in Georgia who's been trying to put President Trump behind bars. But President Trump isn't the only person Fani Willis is abusing her office to target. She's also trying to lock my husband away for the rest of his life for doing his job as a police officer and trying to keep our city safe. - My husband, James, served in the Atlanta Police Department. That's me, James, and our son in the photo I sent. Being a police officer meant the world to James. He was born and raised in Atlanta, and saw his job as a way to help his neighbors. But if Fani Willis has her way... life in prison could be his reward. Our nightmare started more than eight years ago, when an off-duty police officer radioed in about a man breaking into a parked car at an apartment complex. Then the officer stopped responding. Dispatch issued a distress call, and all available units rushed over. My husband was closest to the scene, so he arrived first. When he got there, he saw a running car parked facing the wrong way on a one-way street. With his emergency lights on, James got out to investigate. That's when it happened. The suspect saw my husband, slammed on the gas, and drove straight at him. James was certain the man was intentionally trying to run him over. With only a split second to think, James drew his weapon and fired a single shot at the vehicle bearing down on him. It struck the suspect, who died instantly. James was shaken by the incident, but thankfully he was unhurt. He went home, certain he had done everything by the book. So you can imagine our shock when James found out he was being charged with murder! James was forced to turn himself in, and spent the night locked up in jail in solitary confinement. They treated him like a common criminal. They even kept him in a straitjacket! We all thought this was a giant misunderstanding -- just a soft-on-crime District Attorney trying to make a name for themselves by targeting a police officer. We couldn't have been more wrong. You see, James is white, and the suspect he shot was black. So the woke, anti-police prosecutors in Atlanta have made it their mission to destroy my husband. For eight years now, these radical prosecutors -- and the woke mob who helped get them elected -- have dragged my husband's name through the mud. The District Attorney at the time held press conferences bragging about how tough they were for going after my husband. They even campaigned on putting my James behind bars! Every time James has to appear at the Fulton County Courthouse for a hearing, angry protesters show up carrying giant signs with James' mugshot on them, with “MURDERER” scribbled across his face in red spray paint. Now you understand why I'm so upset and why James is an honest, hardworking, good-hearted man who only wants to help people. He's the guy everyone calls when a broken faucet floods their kitchen, or when their car breaks down. When he was younger, he spent years traveling to dangerous Third-World countries to help needy people -- places like South Sudan, Madagascar, Liberia, and Haiti. And it was that same selflessness that drove James to become a police officer and graduate the police academy near the top of his class. My husband is exactly the kind of man you want keeping your city safe. But none of that matters to radical prosecutors like Fani Willis. She knows what happened that night. She knows James was in a life-or-death situation. She knows that the charge should have been dropped years ago. But Fani Willis doesn't care. She just wants to make an example out of my husband. For years, we've been trapped in limbo. James' police department just threw us away. There's supposed to be this bond of brotherhood in law enforcement. But his fellow officers were forbidden to talk to him -- and the higher-ups fired him before the investigation was even finished. We've lost nearly all our friends, and we've even been forced to move because of the terrible lies the prosecutors have been spreading about my husband. It's been nearly impossible for James to get a job with these charges hanging over him. By some miracle, we've been able to get by, but every day is difficult. And what's been truly overwhelming is the cost of James' legal defense. My friend, we aren't wealthy people. James' father had to empty his retirement account. Other relatives have sold family heirlooms. We've put nearly every penny we can scrape together into this case. And it still hasn't been enough. I know this is how Fani Willis and the radical prosecutors in her office are trying to punish my husband. They're dragging this out as long as possible to break our family. Earlier this year, we ran out of money. James was about to turn to a public defender. We had all but given up hope... And then we got a call from Jason Johnson, the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund, or LELDF. If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongfully-accused police officers like James. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent officers -- and their families – at their lowest moment. And we were at our lowest moment! Not only did the LELDF board decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best attorneys so James can finally clear his name. That's why I'm writing to you today to ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone... His legal team needs to be working day and night to get ready to defeat Fani Willis's team and get my husband declared NOT GUILTY once and for all. I know that times are tough right now. And if we had any other options, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for help from a stranger. But like I mentioned earlier, we have nowhere else to turn. And I'm so scared for what will happen to my husband. You know how police officers are treated behind bars. If James is convicted, it's basically a death sentence. LELDF is a non-profit organization. So your gift is tax-deductible. And it's your that could make the difference between defeating Fani Willis and the radical anti-police prosecutors on her team -- and my husband spending the rest of his life in prison. I'm sorry this e-mail turned out so long. Thank you for taking the time to read about my husband. And thank you in advance for helping my James. Sincerely, Jenna Burns P.S. My husband did EVERYTHING he needed to survive that night. He doesn't deserve to die in prison. But that's EXACTLY what will happen if radical prosecutor Fani Willis gets her way. Please help James before it's too late! Thank you for helping me save my husband and my family. More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visitwww.PoliceDefense.org. And remember -Blue Lives Matter! body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99840218/521028233/-926250309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjAwOTIxZS1iNWNhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=92qaJAot64ahrgvx8t0QH6SO-0_hp-288YnfseFfYbY=&emci=4b00921e-b5ca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&emdi=5200921e-b5ca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=24635485)
Friend, it's Patrick. I’m sure you know, but from January 8 until April 7, the team will be prohibited from all fundraising. Any funds we receive after January 8, we will have to return. That means that we only have **four** more days before we shut down all of our fundraising links and pages, and only **four** more days to reach our pre-session fundraising goal of $17,000. So if you’ve been waiting for the best time to contribute to Wes, this is your chance! **You can make a contribution at this link we created:[_https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250104-1?refcode=20250104-1-dpwillis67@gmail.com_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99840220/521028235/-1354668775?refcode=20250104-1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MjAwOTIxZS1iNWNhLWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTliNDUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=92qaJAot64ahrgvx8t0QH6SO-0_hp-288YnfseFfYbY=)** Having to pause fundraising for three months puts a strain on the team, and we need to make sure that we have resources in place. Fundraising after the holidays is hard, but we know that this team can make it happen. So let’s hit the ground running, and make sure we have a strong start to 2025. clean_body: Friend, it's Patrick. I’m sure you know, but from January 8 until April 7, the team will be prohibited from all fundraising. Any funds we receive after January 8, we will have to return. That means that we only have four more days before we shut down all of our fundraising links and pages, and only four more days to reach our pre-session fundraising goal of $17,000. So if you’ve been waiting for the best time to contribute to Wes, this is your chance! You can make a contribution at this link we created:= Having to pause fundraising for three months puts a strain on the team, and we need to make sure that we have resources in place. Fundraising after the holidays is hard, but we know that this team can make it happen. So let’s hit the ground running, and make sure we have a strong start to 2025. = Thank you for your support, Patrick

name: Pat Herrity
subject: I'm Running for Lieutenant Governor
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Friends of Pat Herrity
body:
|
---
| |
---
| |
---
| Peter,

As the last elected Republican in Fairfax County, I've seen first-hand what happens when liberal politicians are allowed to govern.

Since 2021, Virginia has been on the right track under the commonsense, conservative leadership of Governor Glenn Youngkin. In 2025, it is crucial that we continue that commonsense direction.

**That's why I'm announcing my campaign for Lieutenant Governor of Virginia.**

Alongside Winsome Sears in the Governor's mansion and Jason Miyares as Attorney General, we will continue the commonsense, conservative legacy that Governor Youngkin, Lieutenant Governor Sears, and Attorney General Miyares have established since their victory in 2021.
---
|
---
| [ ](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=919434770b&e=a1ea0f9c99)
---
Watch my announcement video here
|
---
| As a lifelong resident of the Commonwealth, public service and community engagement have always been an important part of my life. I began my career in business, repeatedly guiding small businesses and mid-sized companies to financial success and became a recognized leader in the business, sports and civic communities.

Eventually, I took that service to a new level by running for the Board of Supervisors. Despite the efforts of the liberal Democrat machine in Fairfax County, I successfully won re-election in 2023 in a district where Governor Youngkin received just 38% of the vote.

On the Board of Supervisors, I've been my liberal counterparts' worst nightmare. Not only do I stand in the way of their liberal agenda, **I get things done and deliver commonsense, conservative solutions for the voters who elected me.**

I stopped a meals tax that would have hurt our local residents and businesses. I funded our brave men and women in law enforcement. I reformed the county’s compensation programs to benefit employees and save money for taxpayers. And I was the only elected official to say no when they wanted to silence parents, close schools, and mask kids.

In Richmond, I am eager to continue pursuing the commonsense, conservative solutions that best serve our communities and our Commonwealth. I will lead efforts to lower taxes, support law enforcement, and advocate for our children. I’ll stand up to the liberal prosecutors releasing repeat violent offenders into our neighborhoods. I’ll crack down on localities that refuse to cooperate with ICE to enforce our laws, and I’ll focus education resources on students and quality teachers – not on political agendas.

Republicans can win again in Virginia this year, but it's critical that we improve in Northern Virginia. I've won here for years, and I'll do it again in November for our ticket.

I'm excited to share more with you over the coming months, and I look forward to the opportunity to connect in-person as I travel the Commonwealth! Ultimately, I hope to earn your support as we work to continue the legacy of commonsense, conservative leadership in Richmond.

Thank you,
---
| |
---
---
|
---
**Pat Herrity**
Springfield District Supervisor, Fairfax County
Candidate for Lieutenant Governor

clean_body: | | | | | | Peter, As the last elected Republican in Fairfax County, I've seen first-hand what happens when liberal politicians are allowed to govern. Since 2021, Virginia has been on the right track under the commonsense, conservative leadership of Governor Glenn Youngkin. In 2025, it is crucial that we continue that commonsense direction. That's why I'm announcing my campaign for Lieutenant Governor of Virginia. Alongside Winsome Sears in the Governor's mansion and Jason Miyares as Attorney General, we will continue the commonsense, conservative legacy that Governor Youngkin, Lieutenant Governor Sears, and Attorney General Miyares have established since their victory in 2021. | | Watch my announcement video here | | As a lifelong resident of the Commonwealth, public service and community engagement have always been an important part of my life. I began my career in business, repeatedly guiding small businesses and mid-sized companies to financial success and became a recognized leader in the business, sports and civic communities. Eventually, I took that service to a new level by running for the Board of Supervisors. Despite the efforts of the liberal Democrat machine in Fairfax County, I successfully won re-election in 2023 in a district where Governor Youngkin received just 38% of the vote. On the Board of Supervisors, I've been my liberal counterparts' worst nightmare. Not only do I stand in the way of their liberal agenda, I get things done and deliver commonsense, conservative solutions for the voters who elected me. I stopped a meals tax that would have hurt our local residents and businesses. I funded our brave men and women in law enforcement. I reformed the county’s compensation programs to benefit employees and save money for taxpayers. And I was the only elected official to say no when they wanted to silence parents, close schools, and mask kids. In Richmond, I am eager to continue pursuing the commonsense, conservative solutions that best serve our communities and our Commonwealth. I will lead efforts to lower taxes, support law enforcement, and advocate for our children. I’ll stand up to the liberal prosecutors releasing repeat violent offenders into our neighborhoods. I’ll crack down on localities that refuse to cooperate with ICE to enforce our laws, and I’ll focus education resources on students and quality teachers – not on political agendas. Republicans can win again in Virginia this year, but it's critical that we improve in Northern Virginia. I've won here for years, and I'll do it again in November for our ticket. I'm excited to share more with you over the coming months, and I look forward to the opportunity to connect in-person as I travel the Commonwealth! Ultimately, I hope to earn your support as we work to continue the legacy of commonsense, conservative leadership in Richmond. name: Medicare for All via Our Revolution
subject: This is an all-out narrative war between Bernie and Musk
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1061?refcode=email-20250104_v&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis — Bernie went
on Jon Stewart’s show to counter Elon Musk’s efforts to slash Medicare and
shine a light on America’s deadly, for-profit healthcare system.

What’s the function of the healthcare system? Bernie said it bluntly: “It
is to make the drug companies and insurance companies rich.”

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20832?t=1110&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ][IMG]

Bernie again advocated that Medicare for All would reduce spending on
healthcare (just like Musk wants) while covering everyone and making care
better. M4A will literally save money AND LIVES!

If we’re going to win this fight against the Trump-Musk administration,
protect Medicare, and battle our way towards a future with Medicare for
All, we need the resources to forge ahead, amplify this message, and win
the narrative war.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1107?express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]Can you chip in today to back Bernie & Our Revolution’s work to counter
Musk's efforts to slash the social safety net? We have the answer to high
healthcare costs and it’s MEDICARE FOR ALL!

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1092?express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1093?express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1095?express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1096?express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1097?express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/20831?t=1098?&amount=9&refcode=email-20250104nd&akid=7472%2E5885482%2EYF0jJx ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

On healthcare, Elon Musk asks, “Shouldn’t the American people be getting
their money’s worth?” Our response is YES — Medicare for All would save
over $600 BILLION per year AND save lives. Bernie is grabbing the reins of
the narrative and pushing for the real answer to the deadly, for-profit
American health care system.

Musk wants to cut out administrative middlemen? Great, get rid of private
insurance middlemen and create a single payer system focused on actual
healthcare not enriching CEOs and generating obscene profits that push

name: Ryan Brown via Justice Democrats
subject: Amazon fired me.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

My name is Ryan Brown, I’m the President of Carolina Amazonians United for
Solidarity & Empowerment (CAUSE), based out of the Raleigh-Durham
warehouse (RDU1).

We just filed petitions with the National Labor Relations Board requesting
an election to become the first unionized Amazon fulfillment center in the
South, and only the second in the United States!

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250104-cause?refcode=em20250104-36290&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36290_1203867_-Wd1fQ&akid=36290%2E1203867%2E-Wd1fQ ]Photo of Ryan Brown and other CAUSE organizers

(that’s me on the left)

Amazon must have known this was coming, because last month, they fired me.
It was right before the holiday rush, which means the warehouse was
understaffed during the busiest part of the year.

I was the third CAUSE organizer fired in the past year. We’re not backing
down. We’re going to fight, and we’re going to win.

Amazon workers are riding high on the groundswell of support during the
Amazon Teamsters strike before Christmas. And we can tell you―it has
Amazon scared.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250104-cause?refcode=em20250104-36290&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36290_1203867_-Wd1fQ&akid=36290%2E1203867%2E-Wd1fQ ]CAUSE has been working hand-in-hand with The Labor Force to get to this
point. They have been providing support and resources when no one else
would. Can you split a solidarity donation between Justice Democrats and
The Labor Force to keep powering us through this election?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250104-cause?refcode=em20250104-36290&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=3&refcode2=36290_1203867_-Wd1fQ&akid=36290%2E1203867%2E-Wd1fQ ]Contribute $3

Nothing about this process has been easy. We’ve watched how Amazon treated
worker-organizers in Bessemer, AL and our comrades at Amazon Labor Union.
We’ve been learning lessons. And we’re ready to win.

But every union organizer knows that the best way to win is to have the
support of the whole community behind you. [ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250104-cause?refcode=em20250104-36290&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36290_1203867_-Wd1fQ&akid=36290%2E1203867%2E-Wd1fQ ]Can you split a solidarity
donation with The Labor Force today and help us win?

I want to thank Justice Democrats for their solidarity in amplifying our
message. Together, we will win!

In solidarity,

Ryan Brown
President
Carolina Amazonians United for Solidarity & Empowerment (CAUSE)

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250104-36290-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36290_1203867_-Wd1fQ&akid=36290%2E1203867%2E-Wd1fQ

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36290%2E1203867%2E-Wd1fQ

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
P.O. Box 910 Knoxville, TN 37901
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

clean_body: "YRNF" "YRNF" The Radical Left is doubling down on their anti-Trump lawfare. They're at it again, trying to KNEECAP President Trump and his second term. Chuck Schumer just texted MILLIONS of Far-Left Kamala donors, urging them to support his " No Kings Act." This isn't just another bill. It's a direct attack on President Trump--a scheme to strip him of his presidential immunity and bury his administration in endless sham investigations. Sound familiar? It 's Russia, Russia, Russia all over again. We cannot let them win. We must protect President Trump and the America First agenda at all costs. Here's how you can help: We know it's so soon after the election, but the Radical Left is already working overtime to DERAIL Trump's presidency. Until January 20th, they'll fight tooth and nail to stop him from leading America. They'll NEVER allow President Trump or Republicans to have a productive second term unless we fight back. Patriot, we just overwhelmed the Left at the ballot box on Election Day, but this fight is far from over. We're asking one more time for your support to STOP Chuck Schumer and his SHAMELESS attacks. Can we count on YOU? Every signature we get, every dollar you give sends a clear message: the America First movement is here to stay, and we won 't let the Radical Left destroy our future. Let's finish what we started. Let's defend our victory. Paid for by Eric Burlison for Congress 2135 E. Independence Ave. #1007 Springfield, MO 65807 "Eric Burlison for Congress"

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: It’s time for Medicare for All.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
I’ve said repeatedly that Democrats need to organize around — and deliver on — a bold, populist, working class agenda in order to win.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20250104-4644-footer&t=1&akid=4644%2E420207%2ElfjqU5)
Peter,
I’ve said repeatedly that Democrats need to organize around — and deliver on — a bold, populist, working class agenda in order to win.
**Medicare for All _must_ be a cornerstone of our platform.**
The current situation is completely unsustainable. Corporate greed has taken over the health care system, and with every visit to the emergency room, Americans are at risk of being pushed into severe medical debt.
On top of that, billion-dollar pharmaceutical corporations are raising the prices of prescriptions and making it harder for everyday Americans to stay healthy.
_It doesn’t have to be this way._ Other nations have figured out how to deliver quality care without breaking the bank, and it’s time for us to join them.
[**Will you add your name to my petition and call on fellow Democrats to commit to making Medicare for All a reality?**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/commit-to-m4a-20250104?source=em20250104-4644&akid=4644%2E420207%2ElfjqU5)
**[Add your name](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/commit-to-m4a-20250104?source=em20250104-4644&akid=4644%2E420207%2ElfjqU5)**
Together,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20250104-4644-footer&t=2&refcode2=4644_420207_lfjqU5&akid=4644%2E420207%2ElfjqU5)**

Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

name: COURIER
subject: When Trump takes over, we won’t – we can’t – shut up and give up
body:
We won’t shut up and give up – we’ll stand up and power up

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843261/521036803/2130526953?refcode=emwrapper&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=&emci=34922ffb-1cc9-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=10d2e1ca-bcca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=14016711)
Peter **–**
In the wake of Trump’s re-election, the media has bowed to the MAGA movement, pretending that Trump, his policies, and his fascist tone are the new normal.
This week, COURIER’s own Mark Jacob has a critical message to share: **when Trump takes over, we won’t – we can’t – shut up and give up.**
Now more than ever, it’s time to stand up and power up. At COURIER, we’re doing just that, working to build a progressive media infrastructure that will fight against the right-wing’s lies and conspiracies and the mainstream media’s capitulation.
[**If you have a moment, please read Mark’s message below. And if you’re able, please consider donating $25 or whatever you can to help COURIER build the media network we need during Trump’s second term.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843262/521036804/-593472309?refcode=20250102-EM-MarkJacob3Resend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=)
* * *
_**Here’s what we WON’T do when Trump takes over**_
_We won’t shut up and give up – we’ll stand up and power up_
As democracy defenders, we’re facing hard times when authoritarian Donald Trump takes office Jan. 20. But what will we do about it? For now, I’m focusing on what we won’t do:
We won’t shut up.
We won’t retreat from the news.
We won’t lose our ability to be outraged.
We won’t be duped by a fake “crisis” that serves as a pretext to send the military against American citizens and turn our country into a police state.
We won't sit on our couch and watch protests on TV when we should be out protesting in front of the TV cameras.
We won’t tolerate abuse of women simply because the person who won the last presidential election is a sexual predator.
We won’t get exhausted. Instead, we’ll pace ourselves, find ways to relax and enjoy life, and be ready to go at the crucial moments.
We won’t accept the notion that “all politicians lie.” More politicians lie when the news media and public accept lying and thus make it advantageous to lie.
We won’t forget to be kind.
We won’t expect the New York Times, the Washington Post and the TV networks to wake up and seriously confront the threat of fascism when they didn't do it before the election.
We won’t forget that Trump won by just 1.5 percentage points — not a mandate, and certainly not a statement that most Americans want to surrender their rights to him.
**[Support Independent Journalism](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843263/521036805/-593472309?refcode=20250102-EM-MarkJacob3Resend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=)**
We won't trust anything that Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says about public health.
We won’t let Christofascism make us forget the good acts of Christians. We’ll urge Christians who follow Christ’s teachings to publicly oppose people who call themselves Christians but follow Trump’s teachings instead.
We won’t forget that immigration makes our country richer, both culturally and economically.
We won't backbite other pro-democracy advocates just because we disagree with them on style points and other minor issues.
We won't lose our sense of humor, which sustains us in difficult times.
We won’t accept slander and disrespect toward public workers, who do jobs that benefit us all. In fact, we’ll celebrate any bureaucratic red tape they can create to stall the cruelties that Team Trump attempts.
We won’t pretend that anyone who voted for Trump is a “moderate.”
We won’t stop supporting public education, which creates a more cohesive and equitable society. And we won’t stop fighting against school vouchers that starve public schools while helping well-off parents pay for private schools.
We won't call Trump “the leader of the free world,” and we’ll mock any journalists who do.
We won’t be irrational optimists. We’ll recognize the potential for the worst to happen, based on our understanding of the history of fascism. This will not cripple us – it will motivate us.
We won’t pretend racism is “economic anxiety.”
We won’t admire people simply because they assemble large piles of money. Instead we’ll admire people who do the right things with their money.
We won't give up on American democracy. Instead we’ll remember a famous saying: “Americans can always be counted on to do the right thing – after they’ve exhausted all other possibilities.”
We won’t dismiss the possibility that friends and family who voted for Trumpism will abandon it when they see the damage it does.
We won’t let the Republican culture of hate cause us to hate our fellow human beings.
We won’t give up our values – our sense of right and wrong.
And we won’t lose hope. Because if we stand strong, we won’t lose our country.
We won’t.
**[Donate ➡️](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843264/521036806/-593472309?refcode=20250102-EM-MarkJacob3Resend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=)**
* * *
Email us: [**info@couriernewsroom.com**](mailto:info@couriernewsroom.com)
[**Become a member! **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843265/521036807/1028889122?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=&emci=34922ffb-1cc9-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=10d2e1ca-bcca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=14016711)
This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

[**Receive Fewer Emails**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843266/521036808/1416945388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=&emci=34922ffb-1cc9-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=10d2e1ca-bcca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=14016711)
[**Update Your Information**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/99843267/521036809/1324474970?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMTBkMmUxY2EtYmNjYS1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5YjQ1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AEALdLSmoD1WBIFO8GRzx7xpoYR8tHY-qtW0BDU33bc=&emci=34922ffb-1cc9-ef11-88ce-0022482a93af&emdi=10d2e1ca-bcca-ef11-88d0-0022482a9b45&ceid=14016711)
**[unsubscribe](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/XCUEQN0BTUWWQ1xBmQeS9g2?unsubscribedata=Urjh%2Bcb01j6KNDyGQcCbdxSG9MzRNs%2FHcJmOp6W34wAgtyoEnhCY1YSkr7BuQEUuAK5Uqh96uO6oXOWREF%2F4N3jbneLgj2EenjELPRIj0iqKneqh8bu%2BWNA9rSa3974Q35USOQ6lS2KcsxowWH2wxiXWgSYpv9DSW%2B7DlxuYmuohFjxi1CZ8viD8kcE%2FRPUc%2FAyN3N1i4iLPszwRZ7Q1ijymYEx3RW5V7J%2BKTBh6DVrdB7zOD37hMeaA%2BNJDLX9TardiUsECHbS3%2FntLQc6lD0UDYZBNnZHhb8UkspX4hL4too2MDffjvjfZLajwcya7S3xZm2NlEgFpi78dkF%2F1bmH2y7p%2FBoMYykO7zpEP%2F%2BdrD5Nyd6%2B%2BCPAhGw3CWUH0hbpLii7L98tt5rmJqv8cdaTCs0XgAcavSfDhnHL8sZUfkRBy6YgY5ipEoxhdWgXo)**

# [On the Brink of Anarchy?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=154147340&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTU0MTQ3MzQwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzYwMTAxNTAsImV4cCI6MTczODYwMjE1MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.BU0SMF2STPpmPjNQS3cxdrSIH4ZMj4wOAY0K_swA2vs)
### The changing tide of political violence and what we can do to stem it
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Jan 4
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---

Last week we witnessed a stereotypical terrorist attack in New Orleans: [an ISIS-inspired radical drove a truck into a crowd](https://substack.com/redirect/6863de1b-daa7-4efb-a4b1-df78ffb36b48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) killing a bunch of innocent people and then engaged in a shootout with police before being killed.
A few hours later, a [Tesla Cybertruck full of fireworks and gas canisters](https://substack.com/redirect/f44e9df6-31e9-4429-b09a-bdf2bd0ee0de?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was ignited outside the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas. Investigators [immediately sought ties](https://substack.com/redirect/2f1d8569-d0b6-42c8-95b2-cd0664cb26f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) between the two incidents.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.tgk5LtTPNt9q75dSy8eq8r45_KH687YzdIGyNlev96I?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=154147340)
But the two incidents weren't linked at all. The Cybertruck explosion was carried out by a [37 year old active duty Army Green Beret, Mathew Livelsberger](https://substack.com/redirect/f44e9df6-31e9-4429-b09a-bdf2bd0ee0de?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who spent his life fighting ISIS-inspired terrorists. If anything, the Tesla Cybertruck incident was linked- at least spiritually- to another recent high profile incident: Luigi Mangione's murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
I wrote about [what that assassination reveals about our politics](https://substack.com/redirect/7e69ac43-83a0-419b-9d40-9bc0b744c1c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) last month. But the eerie similarity between both that assassination and the recent Cybertruck explosion is that the acts weren't based on extreme ideology like the ISIS terrorists we are used to. No, the positions that Mangione and Livelsberger cited for their violent acts were, for lack of a better term, _quite normal_.
Sure, the pundits are saying Mangione is deranged and that Livelsberger [was going through a mental health crisis](https://substack.com/redirect/ea665f9b-150f-416b-a6e2-24bb94a7d8d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), so we should just write them off as nutcases and move on. But writing something off as nutty or anomalous is a perilous thing to do, even if it might be true. So instead of doing that, let's look at their self-described positions for their actions:
Mangione:_ "The reality is, these [indecipherable] have simply gotten too powerful, and they continue to abuse our country for immense profit because the American public has allowed them to get away with it.... it is not an issue of awareness at this point, but clearly power games at play. Evidently, I am the first to face it with such brutal honesty."_
Livelsberger: _" We are being led by weak and feckless leadership who only serve to enrich themselves"_ and _" This was not a terrorist attack, it was a wake up call. Americans only pay attention to spectacles and violence. What better way to get my point across than a stunt with fireworks and explosives."_
Does any of this sound familiar? Wealth inequality, corporate greed and violence, the richest and most powerful people taking advantage of everyone else. Again, these aren't ISIS extremists or radicals. These are Ivy League kids and lifelong soldiers with mainstream positions- positions that a vast swath of the public from both parties hold- perhaps reaching crisis points, and then acting on them.
Another thing their acts have in common? It doesn't look like these two intended to threaten the general public. In fact, both appear to have thought they were doing our country and the everyday people living here a favor.
Mangione killed one very specific person to make his statement. Livelsberger also killed one person, himself, in what he called a "spectacle" that was clearly designed to be very flashy but wasn't very destructive.
Some people think Livelsberger's explosion was a failure:
"The level of sophistication is not what we would expect from an individual with this type of military experience," said Kenny Cooper, a special agent in charge for the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives.
Maybe it was a failure. But it's also possible that it worked exactly as he planned: a fiery spectacle that didn't really hurt anyone else but brought attention to his death and grievances. It sure doesn't seem like this guy wanted to be a villain based on what he wrote. He was broken up about civilian casualties in Afghanistan. And this is what his ex-girlfriend had to say about him:
_"I just want everyone to know that Matt was the kindest man I ever knew,"_ [Arritt told the Gazette](https://substack.com/redirect/ce75d983-a86a-4f64-a9dc-71fc984fa1d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), explaining that Livelsberger bought her a house when her mother became ill. "_He got me through a difficult time "_ and _" I never saw him angry or act impulsively and he was always thoughtful in the things he did. He was always the first person to jump into battle and stand up for someone else."_
What he appears to have wanted was what Mangione appears to have wanted, for his life (and in this case death) to make a statement, which is corroborated by the fact that [he sent a letter/manifesto to someone with a platform](https://substack.com/redirect/ea665f9b-150f-416b-a6e2-24bb94a7d8d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) before the incident even happened.
The insanity of all of this mixed in with the current polarized and fervent political climate? America went through a time just like it in the past! Maybe we can learn something from that.
[In the late 1800's](https://substack.com/redirect/3cf9a89b-5cc4-4df8-9540-a4b31f69bcb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) wealth inequality was skyrocketing, workers were organizing for power against an elite profit-maximizing class, and political polarization was at a zenith. As a result, people caught political fever, and it became contentious. Check out how [familiar this description of the late 1800's sounds](https://substack.com/redirect/3cf9a89b-5cc4-4df8-9540-a4b31f69bcb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
_" Such public politics became, in the words of one comedian, "our great American game." Political rancor grew precipitously. Saloons resounded with heated debates. On train cars, Americans took straw polls to see how strangers would vote. At dinner tables, families bonded--or broke up--debating an upcoming race. Even when exhausted Americans threw down their newspapers, they looked up only to find partisan broadsides slathered on every wall. "Ignorance is bliss now," complained one woman as she canceled her political newspapers, weary of the whole spectacle."_
Did you know that the highest sustained participation in elections, something we are seeing a surge in right now, was actually during the late 1800's?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7ce00393-f556-4200-aca6-ca3c21cfe724?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Elections were also closer than ever before. [No president in this period came to office by winning a majority of the popular vote](https://substack.com/redirect/3cf9a89b-5cc4-4df8-9540-a4b31f69bcb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
At the same time, workers who were tired of being abused started fighting for an 8 hour work week. Anarchism and "[propaganda of the deed](https://substack.com/redirect/04d473e8-4d9b-4b9f-b692-a7bac8f7b767?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," like what Mangione and Livelsberger did, proliferated.
And some of them even became martyrs, like Mangione and Livelsberger appear to aspire to. For example, [in 1886](https://substack.com/redirect/7ddcb758-dbe9-45e8-af26-16b934ed3bfc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Chicago, "a rally at Haymarket Square was organized by labor radicals (note: their word, and one often chosen by those who take more than their share to label those who want a fair piece of it) to protest the killing and wounding of several workers by the Chicago police during a strike the day before at the McCormick Reaper Works."

When police arrived at the rally to disperse the crowd, someone threw a bomb at the officers. Mayhem ensued, and in "August 1886, eight men labeled as anarchists were convicted in a sensational and controversial trial in which the jury was considered to be biased and no solid evidence was presented linking the defendants to the bombing."
Most were hanged, but they are remembered, and despite being called anarchists, they are not remembered in a bad light. Quite the opposite, in fact. There is a [memorial to them standing in Chicago today](https://substack.com/redirect/8838315f-d34c-44ce-adfd-3f380658e4d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that is listed on the National Parks Service website.
Political violence was everywhere during this time. On average, a sitting US Congressman was murdered [every seven years](https://substack.com/redirect/3cf9a89b-5cc4-4df8-9540-a4b31f69bcb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Two presidents were assassinated, in 1881 and 1901.
We are there again. Multiple assassination attempts on a (former) President and presidential candidate. Propaganda of the deed proliferating with Mangione and Livelsberger. And political polarization and doubts in democracy everywhere.
Have you heard someone say that democracy is failing because people are stupid? (Come on, you've probably thought that yourself) Or that dumb and uneducated people have more children than smart people so we are doomed?
Here is a quote from an [1878 treatise on the failure of democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/7000277d-9812-429c-a6ee-dd049f2828fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Francis Parkman, an elite of the era, bemoaning the growing democratic power of the lower classes:
_" It is aggravated by the fact, generally acknowledged "by those most competent to judge of it," that intellectual development and high civilization are not favorable to fecundity, so that the unintelligent classes, except when in actual destitution, multiply faster than those above them. Thus the power of ignorance tends to increase, or rather the power of the knaves who are always at hand to use it."_
Jon Grinspan, of the National Museum of American History, [whose article](https://substack.com/redirect/3cf9a89b-5cc4-4df8-9540-a4b31f69bcb9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) I quoted a couple of times above, lists even more similarites:
_" Others attacked the rising immigration rates, like Francis Willard, the leader of the Women's Christian Temperance Union, who blamed America's out-of-control politics on "alien illiterates." Others still aimed (more accurate) attacks at railroads, corporations, robber barons and lobbyists who seemed to be buying up America. The muckraking reporter Henry Demarest Lloyd wrote that "liberty produces wealth, and wealth destroys liberty."_
So how did this era end and is there anything we can learn from it?
According to Jon, the political and voting fervor began to die down as those in charge stifled voting access in order to decrease turnout. Which, you can see in the turnout chart above, appears to have had the intended result.
At the same time, however, labor unions started winning 8 hour workdays left and right, antitrust laws were passed and enforced, and a feeling of fairness started to return the country. In short, normal people started getting results and maybe weren't as interested in politics anymore.
So, if we want to move on from this, and not fall into twenty years of political strife and violence, perhaps the best way to do that is to make things fair again. To get big money out of politics and government, to pass laws so that insurance companies can't [reject bionic arms for little girls](https://substack.com/redirect/cb6db1d2-2650-4b6a-a2ec-34efe3a0c6f8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and otherwise bring a sense of fairness back to America.
Or, maybe some level of anarchy inevitable?
What do you think?
As always, please let me know in the comments, I do read them all! And, also as always, if you have the capacity to support our work, you can [become a paid subscriber here](https://substack.com/redirect/20e74b46-2e9c-44c9-9c3e-72f0cda056b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Until next time.
Lucas

Have you heard someone say that democracy is failing because people are stupid? (Come on, you've probably thought that yourself) Or that dumb and uneducated people have more children than smart people so we are doomed? Here is a quote from an by Francis Parkman, an elite of the era, bemoaning the growing democratic power of the lower classes: " It is aggravated by the fact, generally acknowledged "by those most competent to judge of it," that intellectual development and high civilization are not favorable to fecundity, so that the unintelligent classes, except when in actual destitution, multiply faster than those above them. Thus the power of ignorance tends to increase, or rather the power of the knaves who are always at hand to use it." Jon Grinspan, of the National Museum of American History, I quoted a couple of times above, lists even more similarites: " Others attacked the rising immigration rates, like Francis Willard, the leader of the Women's Christian Temperance Union, who blamed America's out-of-control politics on "alien illiterates." Others still aimed (more accurate) attacks at railroads, corporations, robber barons and lobbyists who seemed to be buying up America. The muckraking reporter Henry Demarest Lloyd wrote that "liberty produces wealth, and wealth destroys liberty." So how did this era end and is there anything we can learn from it? According to Jon, the political and voting fervor began to die down as those in charge stifled voting access in order to decrease turnout. Which, you can see in the turnout chart above, appears to have had the intended result. At the same time, however, labor unions started winning 8 hour workdays left and right, antitrust laws were passed and enforced, and a feeling of fairness started to return the country. In short, normal people started getting results and maybe weren't as interested in politics anymore. So, if we want to move on from this, and not fall into twenty years of political strife and violence, perhaps the best way to do that is to make things fair again. To get big money out of politics and government, to pass laws so that insurance companies can't , and otherwise bring a sense of fairness back to America. Or, maybe some level of anarchy inevitable? What do you think? As always, please let me know in the comments, I do read them all! And, also as always, if you have the capacity to support our work, you can . Until next time. name: Dr. Robert Jeffress
subject: The difference heaven makes today
body:
Imagine you’re planning a move to a new country
| | | [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d0d69b8fa3434bac9373b2b1cbcdfddf&_e=ZiZAVJIXqzITNKl9HTomVRw4Z0CnievR-mVNwUsQAqd1SNh3lpCVgmKgLYbUFcnD_3lUw3mSX7_8k2arPzylQ6owN0NfMXq1qUa3PTTX4fCw2fHuxfdj4awgl4a-EvKNS5UMHs_8qQccOlPK8MuM-pSfcPWz29AozHQ-DV5WhRtFlGcG0VK6ikt-AVUjJayD9XSWXsSdSSv4_9Vh0xUSyVqMwumeV-LZKXYvP1nXvu1jWrh4GE2O3hoK1eNVFpRueqPZQtvmgYW45wZXIe4VwTO-9pq3GALmC7a0YU5S3gErYHcJHK2ox_2VXhjEa5f_oOytI7xo-O-t3z2n7VwCb0tNlpG8q4vIjifNoziV-BB6yZMPpccoFBcoGUtyDHXsFhOUFFLn8G5TGdPLcEhGC5DCueirtzpsE25Zp3uQ3KI%3D)
---
Hello Friend,

Imagine you're planning a move to a new country--a faraway place you've never visited before. Wouldn't you want to know everything you could about it--what the culture is like, the language, the climate, and most importantly, how to get there? It would make a huge difference in how you prepare for the journey!

As Christians, we know that one day we will leave this world and be united with God in heaven. But surprisingly, many of us know very little about what awaits us in that incredible place.
---
| | In my book _A Place Called Heaven_ , I dive into Scripture to answer 10 surprising questions about heaven, including:

* Is heaven a real place or just a state of mind?
* Will we know our loved ones when we get there?
* Do people in heaven know what's happening on earth?
* What difference does a future heaven make in our lives today?

---
| [](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d0d69b8fa3434bac9373b2b1cbcdfddf&_e=ZiZAVJIXqzITNKl9HTomVRw4Z0CnievR-mVNwUsQAqd1SNh3lpCVgmKgLYbUFcnD_3lUw3mSX7_8k2arPzylQ6owN0NfMXq1qUa3PTTX4fCw2fHuxfdj4awgl4a-EvKNS5UMHs_8qQccOlPK8MuM-pSfcPWz29AozHQ-DV5WhRtFlGcG0VK6ikt-AVUjJayD9XSWXsSdSSv4_9Vh0xUSyVqMwumeV-LZKXYvP1nXvu1jWrh4GE2O3hoK1eNVFpRuWhlghptiYMvfE1n0GGJkyH54c90qQaF-8M7BAFzHlbtwCBLoVkYLk1gp2r7NW3YEbzTO9LWFjFkgyKuxvRk3BB_H7_MxcVBf0KucJzSL1Ji7V39ic64BWdNHWASGKp4v8CNf4UhJwwms8K8vUYmC_ATVvDg2rKwHo_wjxfO72pM%3D)
---
| | I know many of us have asked these questions at some point. Understanding what the Bible says about our eternal home doesn't just satisfy our curiosity--it transforms how we live today. By shifting our focus from the temporary struggles of this life to the eternal glory that awaits us, we can live with greater hope, purpose, and peace.

To thank you for your generous gift to _Pathway to Victory_ today, I'd love to send you a copy of _A Place Called Heaven_ , [https://secure.ptv.org/a-place-called-heaven-generous-gift-1](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d0d69b8fa3434bac9373b2b1cbcdfddf&_e=ZiZAVJIXqzITNKl9HTomVRw4Z0CnievR-mVNwUsQAqd1SNh3lpCVgmKgLYbUFcnD_3lUw3mSX7_8k2arPzylQ6owN0NfMXq1qUa3PTTX4fCw2fHuxfdj4awgl4a-EvKNS5UMHs_8qQccOlPK8MuM-pSfcPWz29AozHQ-DV5WhRtFlGcG0VK6ikt-AVUjJayD9XSWXsSdSSv4_9Vh0xUSyVqMwumeV-LZKXYvP1nXvu1jWrh4GE2O3hoK1eNVFpRuiSa1n3j3HKxF_-JM4Q30Fnp4hd2kcBns8rRK6AJrgRNDIpX-CeL5vRXohugKC2E7n4YeYXUFQ2FhB0LOKh8OWeqHP0JDoWG3eSP-OhnGuQ5oq0joJz8k5JyOSLq2ENSkIFbQ4wJBPrfdMfO8wQ63HY4kGc2l5Dc_cDwHtX4FbTk%3D)

I hope this book will give you a clearer picture of the future God has prepared for you and help you live each day with eternity in mind.

Thank you for your continued support, which helps us spread the gospel of Jesus Christ to a world in need of hope.

Thank you for standing with us and sharing the gospel of Jesus Christ.

Sincerely,

Dr. Robert Jeffress

P.S. - For your gift of $75 or more, we'll send you _A Place Called Heaven_ , the beautiful gift book _Encouragement from A Place Called Heaven_ , our exclusive ministry brochure, and the companion message series on DVD and MP3 audio. Click here to get a copy of this spiritually uplifting bundle: [https://secure.ptv.org/encouragement-from-a-place-called-heaven-large-kit-1](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=d0d69b8fa3434bac9373b2b1cbcdfddf&_e=ZiZAVJIXqzITNKl9HTomVVQgtABdWaj23yfhu-1WW1AxxgQ-jfOt3SMhwig6T8UrQqgLHCKcnD8VNjaLS8FR5ce_yZAPiNKDNd3XYV4Ivy4rV8_HCyYZ1DJVM_CpUUjnDQ3_jnhluj01f9d0OY8Gy2xRb4sB9Q7WVNyqDNqQ6AoSjgsHVUQaOT44xEeuQkFUq35a0_V4IrAyD2SN5YYVuvB4RrKIgp41kQMVObQqRcDUUxYwi1eO6IvnbEFIbqRXAgtERdnwoeYawC13cny3FTK7vBEDmSWhYawiXV1h4nE-kY8zhIVeVsbJsQS7wUtw3aqHvu33aG-eXTT5-CNZO1L029aNjHGzoYOw0hGXkfLX_xgQIrpvC94I5ATfd5-rJLQnN3e6vhxtAb8uVe0NBmqOFJNKSMF-UZkZ9pIEayRyZimnH1s93sd0BiXqAIII)

name: Emily Randall
subject: It's official: I'm Congresswoman Randall!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
Thank you, again, for everything you did to make this possible. I cannot thank you enough — and I feel ready for the challenges ahead because I know we’ll face them together.

Emily Randall for Congress | Peter — it’s official. Yesterday, with loved ones by my side, I was sworn in as a member of the 119th Congress from Washington’s 6th District. I have a lot to say, but I’ll do my best to keep this short: It’s the honor of my life to serve my neighbors and follow in the footsteps of hard-working public servants like Derek Kilmer and Norm Dicks – and like my hospital housekeeper grandma Della Trujillo Randall, like the teachers and civil servants and community leaders who are an example to all of us. Along with my colleagues in Congress, I’ll be tackling many of our country’s most immense challenges: bringing down household costs, strengthening workers’ rights, addressing the climate crisis, protecting our healthcare and safety net from cuts, and so much more. I recognize the responsibility and privilege of being the first — but definitely not the last! — queer Latina in Congress. As a working-class woman who was the first in my family to graduate from college, my hope is that my presence here sends the unmistakable message that ALL of us are welcome, ALL of us deserve respect, and ALL of us have a role to play in building a brighter future. Thank you, again, for everything you did to make this possible. I cannot thank you enough — and I feel ready for the challenges ahead because I know we’ll face them together. In service, Congresswoman Emily Randall clean_body: Thank you, again, for everything you did to make this possible. I cannot thank you enough — and I feel ready for the challenges ahead because I know we’ll face them together. Emily Randall for Congress | Peter — it’s official. Yesterday, with loved ones by my side, I was sworn in as a member of the 119th Congress from Washington’s 6th District. I have a lot to say, but I’ll do my best to keep this short: It’s the honor of my life to serve my neighbors and follow in the footsteps of hard-working public servants like Derek Kilmer and Norm Dicks – and like my hospital housekeeper grandma Della Trujillo Randall, like the teachers and civil servants and community leaders who are an example to all of us. Along with my colleagues in Congress, I’ll be tackling many of our country’s most immense challenges: bringing down household costs, strengthening workers’ rights, addressing the climate crisis, protecting our healthcare and safety net from cuts, and so much more. I recognize the responsibility and privilege of being the first — but definitely not the last! — queer Latina in Congress. As a working-class woman who was the first in my family to graduate from college, my hope is that my presence here sends the unmistakable message that ALL of us are welcome, ALL of us deserve respect, and ALL of us have a role to play in building a brighter future. Thank you, again, for everything you did to make this possible. I cannot thank you enough — and I feel ready for the challenges ahead because I know we’ll face them together. In service, Congresswoman Emily Randall = | | Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress Emily Randall for Congress P.O. Box 1883 Port Orchard, WA 98366 United States | | If you would like to receive only our most important messages, .
